summary: Verified the rewritten EXPORT and DELETE flows for the web preview. Found 2 wiring bugs and fixed them; after the fix all 6 test cases pass: ExportSheet renders in Reader and Editor with all 5 format testIDs + cancel; window.confirm fires on web for delete-book-btn (editor) and book-card long-press (library) with correct message; library loads cleanly; AI backend reachable.
action_items: Self-verify the two minor fixes I applied — they are safe wiring fixes but worth a smoke-test re-read by main agent.

Optional: also wire the Library import error path to use a web-safe alert (currently still uses Alert.alert at lines 119/140/162 of app/(tabs)/index.tsx — no-op on web). Not blocking the export/delete request.
Optional: editor's askAI() and reader's noNavigation flows still call Alert.alert (no-op on web). Convert to window.alert/Toast for consistency.
critical_code_review_comments: reader/[id].tsx was importing exportBook (now unused after switch to ExportSheet) and rendering <ExportSheet/> WITHOUT importing it — caused immediate red-screen 'ExportSheet is not defined' on every Reader open.

editor/[id].tsx export-btn (in meta sheet) was still calling Alert.alert with format options instead of opening the already-imported and already-rendered <ExportSheet/>. On web this was a no-op, breaking the spec.
Library import flow still uses Alert.alert for empty/EPUB/error notifications — these are silent on web.

delete_flows_dialog_confirmed: editor delete-book-btn → window.confirm fired with message: 'Delete "The Quiet Room"?\n\n"The Quiet Room" will be removed permanently. This cannot be undone.'
library book-card-0 long-press → window.confirm fired with message: 'Delete "The Quiet Room"?\n\nThis book will be removed permanently from your device.'

import_btn_static_check: import-btn in app/(tabs)/index.tsx calls DocumentPicker.getDocumentAsync with type=['text/plain','text/markdown','text/x-markdown','application/epub+zip','.txt','.md','.epub'] — no '*/*' wildcard, so Android Chrome's file input will NOT show Camera option. Verified.
seed_data_creation: None — the app self-seeds 'The Quiet Room' when the library is empty (via getBooks() in app/(tabs)/index.tsx).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Reader and Editor now share the same ExportSheet modal (src/components/ExportSheet.tsx) with stable testIDs export-{pdf|epub|docx|md|txt|cancel}. Delete confirmations are window.confirm on web — use page.on('dialog', lambda d: d.accept()) before clicking delete buttons. Two no-op Alert.alert calls remain in library import (informational toasts only) — not blocking, but worth converting if user reports silent failures on import.
rca_of_issue: USER-REPORTED BUG #1 (export does nothing useful): Root cause was that Reader's <ExportSheet> JSX referenced an unimported symbol — every navigation to Reader threw a red-screen ReferenceError before the export button could even be rendered. Editor's export-btn instead used Alert.alert which is a no-op on react-native-web, so tapping it appeared as 'nothing happens'. Fix: added the missing import in reader/[id].tsx and replaced Alert.alert with setShowExport(true) in editor/[id].tsx. USER-REPORTED BUG #2 (delete does nothing): confirmAction in src/lib/dialogs.ts already correctly switches to window.confirm on web — delete works as soon as the surrounding red-screen is fixed. Verified end-to-end: the confirm dialog fires with the correct title/message string for both library long-press and editor delete-book-btn.
